revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = National Historic Site | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located in a rice field on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped kofun with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone chamber with stone sarcophagus. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably narrower. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (beads, [[magatama]], glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century.
The site is open to the public as an archaeological park.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page]

The site is open to the public as an archaeological park.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped circular-type ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

[[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue ware]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Artifacts from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Artifacts from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Artifacts from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Artifacts from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page]
]] ]] ]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page]
]] ]] ]]
