revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

]] ]] ]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page] ]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page] ]] {{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

{{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[en:Mizushina Kofun Group]][[ja:水科古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549051]] Wikidata name: Mizushina Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]].

==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a [[:en:Paddy field|rice field]] on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field}} on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|WD=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|WD=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field}} on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|WD=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|WD=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|WD=Q7420760|1=Sanwa-ku}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice field|1=Paddy field}} on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|WD=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station|1=Takada Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|WD=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, funkyu.jpg|No.11 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-11, sekishitsu.jpg|No11 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-14, funkyu.jpg|No14 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-19, funkyu.jpg|No.19 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, funkyu.jpg|No.21 File:Mizushina Kofun Group-21, sekishitsu.jpg|No.21 burial chamber File:Mizushina Kofun Group-30, sekishitsu.jpg|No.30 burial chamber File:水科古墳群出土品-1.JPG|burial goods from No.11 File:水科古墳群出土品-2.JPG|burial goods from No.19・No. 24 File:水科古墳群出土品-3.JPG|burial goods from No.21 File:水科古墳群出土品-4.JPG|burial goods from No.21 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=146 Jōetsu tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-jpn-sanwa007.html Jōetsu city official home page] ]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|qid=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station|1=Takada Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|qid=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mizushina Kofun Group | native_name =水科古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Mizushina kofun.jpg; File:Mizushina kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mizushina Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|41|N|138|21|05|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.094806|N|138.351306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|qid=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Sanwa-ku|qid=Q7420760|1=Sanwa-ku}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 101,160 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mizushina Kofun Group'''|水科古墳群|Mizushina Kofun-gun}} is a group of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in the Mizushina neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|qid=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190572 |title=水科古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mizushina Kofun cluster is located in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|qid=Q842623|lt=rice field|1=Paddy field}} on the western edge of the Takada Plain, near the alluvial delta of the Inada River. Within a very small area (approximately one hectare) is a group of 34 circular-shaped {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) ''kofun'' with flattened tops. Each has a diameter of seven to ten meters, with the largest in the cluster (Mound No. 21) having a diameter of 20 meters and height of three meters, with a circumferential moat. Only a few of the tombs have been found to have ''[[fukiishi]]''. Nine of the tombs have been excavated, and were found to contain a lateral stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The burial chambers were 4.5 meters long on average, with the longest being 8.6 meters, and had a width of 0.7 to 1.0 meter, although a few were considerably smaller. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included straight swords, daggers, jewellery (cylindrical beads, ''[[magatama]]'', glass balls, gold and silver rings) and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed in the early 7th century, or towards the end of the Kofun period.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], with many of the artifacts discovered stored and displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum'''|牧歴史民俗資料館}} . It is located 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|qid=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station|1=Takada Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|qid=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.
